posts.name: Mauro Dinardo
posts.text:
Dear experts,
I would like to know whether performing a 2D binned likelihood fit with Combine is possible.
The pdf should be an analytic formula (not a histogram template)
The 2D space is, for example, an invariant mass and a DNN score.
Moreover, I would like to compute limits on the cross-section as a function of the invariant mass:
basically very much like in the attached plot used just as an example.
The limit for every mass point should be calculated using a likelihood whose pdf is defined on the aforementioned 2D space.
Many thanks,

Mauro.

Screenshot 2022-06-14 at 9.49.41 PM1196×868 139 KB
posts.cooked_html:
Dear experts,
I would like to know whether performing a 2D binned likelihood fit with Combine is possible.
The pdf should be an analytic formula (not a histogram template)
The 2D space is, for example, an invariant mass and a DNN score.
Moreover, I would like to compute limits on the cross-section as a function of the invariant mass:
basically very much like in the attached plot used just as an example.
The limit for every mass point should be calculated using a likelihood whose pdf is defined on the aforementioned 2D space.

Many thanks,

  • Mauro.

Screenshot 2022-06-14 at 9.49.41 PM1196×868 139 KB

posts.name: Andrea Carlo Marini
posts.text:
Hi,
yes, it is possible. You need to construct the RooDataHist over the two observables and the pdfs (over the observables) should be a function of the truth invariant mass.
The test-statistic and the commands after the datacard is made are the usual ones.
Cheers,
-Andrea
